entries.summary: Phase 1 established the database foundation: added 7 Rust crates + 1 npm dependency, created the full 9-table SQLite schema with 98 metric columns and 3 performance indexes, and wired database initialization into the Tauri app lifecycle with WAL mode, connection pooling via Arc<Mutex<Connection>>, and managed state. All tasks passed review with minor corrections (visibility, schema fixes).

metadata.plan_id: FM ValueScout — CSV Parser & Scouting View

top_rejection_reasons: Schema columns incomplete vs spec (missing raw footedness, starts/subs, second_nationality)
Nullable column in composite PK causing data integrity risk
Struct visibility too restrictive (AppState not pub(crate))
lessons_learned: Coder config-zone block requires self-edit fallback after 3 failures even with valid scope
Review Cargo.lock/resolved versions before claiming a crate version exists
SQLite composite PKs with nullable columns silently allow duplicate rows
Always review schema against spec FRs for missing columns (foot_rawn/start/subs caught by reviewer)
Add tauri::Manager import for app.path() and app.manage() — common Rust newcomer oversight
